title: rubus hedycarpus subsp. pubescens
intro: Rubus hedycarpus subsp. pubescens, also known as the California blackberry or yellow fruiting blackberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus genus and is native to the southwestern region of North America. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anthelmintic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus hedycarpus subsp. pubescens include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable plant in traditional herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
